rationale: Calibration source is the canary-proxy receipt at bench/out/r3-1-31b-manifest-simfabric-canary-proxy-calibration/receipt.json (sha256 8f6cbbfb0812fec7d061375b2d988440820b93054019973e61ac3c7b5401554c). Per-pattern cycles-per-call were derived from (a) bench/out/csl-real-canary-compile/<kernel>/scratch/sim_stats.json files (8x3 fabric, ~14 simulated tiles, 7 canary kernels covering gather/rms_norm/fused_gemv/lm_head_gemv/attention_decode patterns) and (b) bench/out/r3-1-31b-multi-token-decode/scratch-subprocess/step000/scratch_<kernel>/sim_stats.json files (11x3 fabric, ~17 simulated tiles, 2 additional kernels covering kv_write and sample patterns). 9 samples total. Median bytesPerCycle = 0.00445. Ceilings raised to 1.5x the rung-2 receipt's grandPredictedCycles for the largest currently-modeled graph: Qwen 3.6 27B (rung-2 receipt at bench/out/r3-2-27b-manifest-simfabric-predicted-wallclock/budget.json computed against the same throughput-config; grandPredictedCycles=392,726,830, prefill=197,373,216, decode=195,353,614). 1.5x of those values = grand 590M, prefill 297M, decode 294M. Gemma 4 31B's rung-2 prediction (207,349,996 grand; prefill=103,966,848; decode=103,383,148) sits well under these ceilings (~35% utilization) so the bumped values do not affect its launch gate. Per-model ceilings (ceilingsByModel) are a tracked follow-up to land alongside the Qwen evidence packet so each model's headroom is attributed precisely; until then the ceiling is the global max across known models. Replace this calibrationStatus with the sha256 of a real manifest-shape rung-3 dispatch once hardware execution lands (R3-1 / R3-3) and supersedes the canary-proxy calibration.
